name: nightly-refactor
description: Simplification while you sleep: a scout picks one proven-safe refactor target each night, the surgeon executes with a behavior-equivalence baseline, and a review queue waits for you in the morning. Nothing merges without you.

nodes.label: Simplification scout
nodes.promptContext: Repurposed as a refactor scout: find exactly ONE high-value, low-risk simplification — duplication with 3+ call sites, dead code with zero references, an abstraction with a single implementor. Spec it with the evidence (reference counts, call-site list). Explicitly skip anything touching error handling, concurrency, or security paths.

nodes.label: Execute
nodes.promptContext: Execute REFACTOR_SPEC.md exactly. Your baseline-capture step is the whole point of running at night — take the time to record real outputs, not just test results.

nodes.label: Equivalence check
nodes.promptContext: Single question: is this provably behavior-preserving? Re-run the surgeon's baseline yourself. Any output delta, however cosmetic, is a REJECT — the surgeon's contract is byte-identical.
